tavily.answer: Cuba is facing an escalating humanitarian crisis amid tightened U.S. sanctions and an energy blockade. The Cuban government has condemned new U.S. sanctions as economic warfare, warning of deepening humanitarian issues. Fuel shortages have worsened since the U.S. captured former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, leaving Cuba without its main oil supplier. A humanitarian aid ship from Mexico and Uruguay brought 1,700 tons of food, milk, and hygiene supplies to Havana, highlighting the severe shortages and economic crisis. Ships from Mexico, Belize, and Colombia continue to deliver humanitarian aid to Cuba, but fuel shipments remain curtailed, exacerbating the crisis. The U.S. is also pursuing an indictment against former Cuban President Raúl Castro over a 1996 shoot-down, further straining U.S.-Cuba relations.
